address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

summary.p:
This MS. is fully described on pp. 440-444 of my Catalogue and Description,
where references are given to other places where its authorship and contents are
discussed. I copied out the whole text (amounting to 283 large pages) and collated
it throughout with the British Museum MS. Or. 2942 (obtained from Mr Sidney
Churchill on Oct. 10, 1885), noting all variants. This work I completed on April
11, 1891, intending to publish it, but I only published the English translation, under
the title of The Tarikh-i-Jadid or New History of Mirad All Muhammad the
Báb...with an Introduction, Illustrations and Appendices (Cambridge University
Press, 1893). The discovery of Hajji Mirza Jáni of Káshán's Nuqtatu'l-Kaf, on
which the Ta'rikk-i-Jadid is based, in the Bibliothèque Nationale at Paris, rendered
the publication of the latter relatively unimportant, and the text of the former
ultimately appeared, with a full Introduction and Apparatus Criticus, in 1910 as
vol. xv of the "E. J. W. Gibb Memorial" Series. Full accounts of these two books
and their relation to one another will be found in the above-mentioned volumes.
My transcript, originally intended for the Press, is preserved amongst my MSS.,
but I have not at present assigned a class-mark to it. In size it is a (12).

Browne's fuller 1892 catalogue entry devotes most of its space to the work's compilation and authorship rather than to this specific copy's contents, drawing on his own examination of related Bábí manuscripts at the Bibliothèque Nationale in Paris (brought back from Persia by Comte de Gobineau) and on information relayed to him by Baron V. Rosen from Lieutenant A. G. Toumansky's inquiries among the Bábís of 'Ishqábád.

msItem.title: Tārīkh-i jadīd
تاريخ جديد
msItem.note: Browne argues the work is in large part based on an earlier, contemporary history of the Bábí movement by Ḥájí Mírzá Jání of Káshán (martyred at Tehran in 1852), which he believed he had identified among the Gobineau manuscripts at the Bibliothèque Nationale (MSS Suppl. Pers. 1070 and 1071).

Quoting a long communication from Baron Rosen relaying Lieutenant Toumansky's inquiries (conducted via Mírzá Abu'l-Fażl of Gulpáygán, then resident at Samarkand), Browne reports that the work was commissioned by Manakjí Ṣáḥib, written up by Mírzá Ḥusayn Hamadání (d. Rasht, 1299 AH/1881-2) from Manakjí's notes and recollections, structured chronologically on the advice of Mírzá Abu'l-Fażl (who also wrote a preface), and given final redaction by Sayyid Jawád of Karbalá (d. Kirmán, 1299 AH/1881-2, aged about 100). Browne notes the difficulty of reconciling Sayyid Jawád's known loyalty to Ṣubḥ-i-Ezel with the finished work's pro-Bahá'í sympathies, and suggests the relevant passages may be later interpolations or excisions by a copyist.

listBibl.bibl: Recension of the Nuqṭat al-Kāf نقطة الكاف of Jānī, Mirza, d. 1852 حاجى ميرزا جانى . See Or. 1329

msItem.bibl: See sub classmark BBP. 5 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Jul., 1892): 433-499.

See also: Browne, E. G. (1892). Some Remarks on the Bábí Texts Edited by Baron Victor Rosen in Vols. I and VI of the Collections Scientifiques de l’Institut des Langues Orientales de Saint-Pétersbourg. The Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, 259–335. http://www.jstor.org/stable/25197092. p. 318

handDesc.handNote: Written in legible Persian ta'líq of an unpretentious character, by a scribe whose frequent orthographic mistakes suggest, per Browne, "a man of no great education."

history.provenance: Given to E. G. Browne by the Bábís of Shíráz in April 1888.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: Summary, physical description and provenance copied from R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932; further expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábī Manuscripts," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Jul. 1892, pp. 440-444.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Syed Gillani Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBP_5.xml): normalized the altIdentifier from "BBP. 5." to "BBP. 5"; replaced the placeholder physical description (which incorrectly gave "Ff. 12," likely a mis-extraction, with unknown dimensions) with Browne's real physical description (189 folios, 21 x 13 cm, 19 lines, ta'líq hand); added item notes on the work's compilation/authorship and a provenance sentence (received from the Bábís of Shíráz in April 1888) drawn from Browne's fuller 1892 JRAS account (pp. 440-444). Person authority key reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne). Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer for this addition.
Values generated from legacy catalogue using a combination of AI and manual editing.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Julian Cook Changed person and work ids (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
